text: Planning a trip is always exciting. Last summer, I traveled to Japan. Before going, I researched popular spots and learned a few basic phrases. New cultures amazed me—from quiet temples to busy city streets. The food was unforgettable, especially sushi and ramen. However, language barriers sometimes made ordering tricky. Even so, using gestures and a smile worked well. These challenges turned into funny memories. Now, looking back, I realize travel isn’t just about seeing places—it’s about understanding the world and ourselves. Every journey creates lasting memories.
